7xg4 is a multicomponent CRISPR complex; 6n40 is a single-chain membrane transporter, so this is likely a hard negative. I’ll quantify that rigorously with chain-level Kabsch/TM-style comparisons, a complex-level best-map result, and figures showing why the apparent best hit is still weak.

The raw sequence-global alignment inflates residue identity because unrelated proteins with long ungapped alignments still share many same-letter matches by chance. The decisive metrics here are TM-score and RMSD after superposition; I’m writing the report to make that distinction explicit and avoid over-interpreting the 32–56% sequence identity numbers.
